page: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is an x86_64 server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin physical cores running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 32 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. Local storage is provided via a bundled 960 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU is present on this configuration.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng single-core score of 3622.78 and exceptional single-threaded compression speeds, including 39.34 GB/sec in gzip and 17.66 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall into the average tier, with a PassMark CPU score of 34225.15 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 28925.37. Memory performance is strong, scoring 2803.28 in PassMark memory tests, while LLM inference speeds for small 135M models reach top-tier levels at 443.84 tokens/sec for text generation.

Because the 16 vCPUs are allocated on a shared basis, there may be resource contention tradeoffs compared to dedicated core instances. However, this shared architecture provides a cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring high resource density, such as 64.0 GB of RAM and 960 GB of fast NVMe storage, without the premium of dedicated hardware. This configuration is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference or text processing tasks.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is a general-purpose x86_64 server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin cores running at 2.0 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 960 GB NVMe SSD. While its shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs, it offers a highly cost-efficient profile for resource-dense workloads. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong memory performance. Multi-core operations, database tasks, and web serving throughput align with average tier standards. This server is highly suited for general-purpose workloads, database hosting, static web serving, and lightweight LLM inference.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is an x86_64 server with 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin cores, 64 GB RAM, and 960 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance for general-purpose and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s features 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin cores, 64 GB RAM, and 960 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for general-purpose and database tasks.
tagline: A resource-dense shared instance featuring AMD EPYC processors and fast NVMe storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin cores with 64.0 GB RAM
Bundled 960 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Strong memory performance and fast small-model LLM inference
Best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and databases
